summary: The first_proof_1 benchmark of 10 research-level problems shows that 7 of 10 (q1, q2, q3, q7, q8, q9, q10) have a single resolved proof issue and appear effectively closed, while q4, q5, and q6 concentrate nearly all active work. q6 is closest to completion (1 open, 15 resolved), q5 remains contested (14 open, 10 resolved), and q4 is the most challenging with 22 open and 23 resolved issues.
problems: q4 carries the largest open workload (22 open issues), including limiting arguments from simple to general roots, an implicit-differentiation lemma, and a dimensional error (JJ* vs J*J) in the Hessian.
q5 has 14 open issues centered on a converse direction, connectivity bookkeeping, well-definedness of chi^O(H) as a minimum, and a 'discrete slices' conclusion that does not follow.
q6 has a single remaining open issue: a missing rank argument that the top-sigma bar / barrier-update lemma relies on (implicit rank <= s).
Recurring open-issue patterns: gaps in generalization/limiting arguments, well-definedness and minimality justifications, and rank/dimension bookkeeping errors in linear-algebraic steps.
highlights: 7 of 10 problems (q1, q2, q3, q7, q8, q9, q10) are fully resolved with no open issues.
q6 is one resolved rank argument away from completion (15 resolved vs 1 open).
q4 and q6 show strong resolution momentum, having already closed 23 and 15 issues respectively.
Open issues are well-categorized and specific, making them directly actionable.

suggested_todos.title: Close out q6 by supplying the missing rank argument (top-sigma bar / barrier-update lemma, rank <= s)
Fix the dimensional error in q4 (JJ* vs J*J in the Hessian) and verify the implicit-differentiation lemma
Resolve the q4 limiting argument from simple roots to general roots
Establish well-definedness of chi^O(H) as a minimum and repair the 'discrete slices' conclusion in q5
Audit q5 connectivity bookkeeping to triage remaining 14 open issues
Spot-check the 7 resolved problems for completeness before final sign-off
